file.content:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions (must have test_<name>.c; links against toolchain libm)
FUNCS_DOUBLE = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh tanh \
               log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
               acosh atanh expm1 log1p erf erfc lgamma \
               j0 j1 jn y0 y1 yn hypot remainder remquo rint round lrint \
               lround trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions: build ref ELF only (no assembly optimization)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@printf "  %-14s RUN\n" $(1)
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size: $(ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

content.text: `cos` is in `FUNCS_DOUBLE`, not `FUNCS_SINGLE`. Double-precision functions have no assembly optimization - there's nothing to compare against, so `FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE` just runs the ref ELF and prints `RUN` (line 106). `PASS`/`FAIL` only applies to single-precision functions where opt vs ref outputs are diffed.

content.text:
`s_sin.s` is the double-precision `sin()` assembly implementation. The `s_` prefix means double-precision standard function (vs `sf_` for single/float).

It exports three symbols: `sin`, `_sin`, and `__gf_dpsin`. The algorithm:

1. Calls `__gf_dpreduct` - argument reduction (maps input to [-pi/4, pi/4] range, returns quadrant info)
2. Calls `__gf_exmul` - extended-precision multiply
3. Based on quadrant (`x12` bits): if quadrant requires cos path, calls `__gf_sigmax` with `__gf_dpsincon` (sin coefficients); otherwise calls it with `__gf_dpcoscon` (cos coefficients)
4. Packs the extended-precision result back into a 64-bit double via bit manipulation at `.LBret`

message.content: but you just said "Double-precision functions have no assembly optimization"

content.text: You're right - I contradicted myself. `s_sin.s` exists and is a double-precision assembly implementation. Let me check what's actually in `meson.build` to see if it's included in the build.

`s_sin.s` is included in the meson build (line 197) for soft-float RV32 configs. My earlier statement was wrong - double-precision assembly optimizations do exist in this codebase. The Makefile's `FUNCS_DOUBLE` test targets just don't do opt-vs-ref comparison because the Makefile doesn't wire up the double-precision assembly objects for linking - it only links single-precision (`sf_`) asm objects.

message.content: make a plan to fix it

# Code Size Optimization Plan — RISC-V Soft-Float libm Assembly

## Phase 1: Quick Wins (High Impact, Low Risk)


### 1.3 Tail-call conversions
- Some functions end with `call helper; lw ra; addi sp; ret`
- If the call is the last action, convert to `tail helper` (or `j helper`)
- Eliminates need to save/restore `ra` and potentially the entire stack frame
- **Files to audit**: sf_atan.s, sf_exp.s, sf_log.s, sf_log10.s, sf_sqrt.s
  (thin wrappers around andes_* helpers)
- **Savings**: ~4-8 bytes per converted call site

---

## Phase 2: Targeted Refactoring (Medium Impact)

### 2.1 Eliminate redundant `__clzsi2` calls
- sf_fmod.s calls `__clzsi2` three times
- sf_ldexp.s calls `__clzsi2` twice
- Cache CLZ result in a saved register instead of recomputing
- **Savings**: ~12-20 bytes per file

### 2.2 sf_fpclassify.s — reuse repeated constants
- Loads `lui #524288` (0x80000) three times, other large constants repeated
- Load once into a register and reuse throughout
- **Savings**: ~10-15 bytes

### 2.3 Consolidate sf_ceil.s / sf_floor.s
- Both files (~57-59 lines) share nearly identical exponent extraction and
  mantissa manipulation logic
- Option A: Extract shared helper routine
- Option B: Implement ceil in terms of floor: `ceil(x) = -floor(-x)` for
  non-integer values
- **Savings**: ~30-40 bytes combined

### 2.4 Remove redundant `mv` chains around helper calls
- Pattern in sf_sin.s, sf_cos.s, sf_tan.s, others:
  ```
  mv x9, x10       # save arg
  call helper
  mv x10, x9       # restore (sometimes dead after)
  ```
- Reorganize register allocation to pass arguments directly
- ~6 occurrences across codebase
- **Savings**: ~2-4 bytes per occurrence

---

## Phase 3: Structural Optimization (Higher Impact, Higher Risk)

### 3.1 Merge sin/cos shared logic
- sf_sin.s (106 lines) and sf_cos.s (105 lines) are structurally near-identical
- Both call `__gf_fpreduct` then evaluate polynomials with different coefficient
  tables (`__gf_fpsincof` / `__gf_fpcoscof`)
- A unified sincos core with a phase offset parameter could cut combined size ~40%
- **Risk**: must preserve accuracy; needs full test-all validation
- **Savings**: ~50-80 bytes combined

### 3.2 Reduce stack frame sizes in complex functions
- sf_tanh.s: 96-byte frame (largest)
- sf_pow.s: 96-byte frame
- sf_sinh.s: 64-byte frame
- Audit actual register liveness — some saved registers may not need saving
- **Savings**: ~8-16 bytes per function where frame shrinks

### 3.3 Share exp computation in hyperbolic functions
- sf_cosh.s, sf_sinh.s, sf_tanh.s all call `__gf_fpexexp` with similar setup
- Could factor a shared hyperbolic helper that computes exp(x) once and derives
  cosh/sinh/tanh from it
- **Risk**: changes calling convention of internal helpers
- **Savings**: ~40-60 bytes across the three files

---

## Validation

Every change must pass:
```sh
make clean && make test-all
```

For phases 2-3, also verify with:
```sh
make size    # before and after, compare .text sizes
```

---

Here's the plan:

## Fix: Add double-precision opt-vs-ref comparison to Makefile

### Problem
`FUNCS_DOUBLE` uses `FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE` which only runs the ref ELF and prints `RUN`. But 32 of those functions have `s_*.s` assembly that IS in the meson build for soft-float RV32 - so we could be doing real opt-vs-ref comparison.

### Split FUNCS_DOUBLE into two groups

**FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT** - have `s_*.s`, get full PASS/FAIL treatment:
`sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo`

**FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT** - no assembly, keep `RUN`:
`acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma j0 j1 jn y0 y1 yn hypot remainder lrint lround`

### Changes to Makefile

1. **Add `S_ASM_SRCS`** - list of `s_*.s` objects (parallel to `ASM_SRCS` for sf_)
2. **Add `FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT`** macro - mirrors `FUNC_RULES_SINGLE` but:
   - asm object name is `s_$(1).o` (no suffix stripping needed, function name = base name)
   - links opt ELF with the `s_*.o` object
   - diffs ref vs opt, prints PASS/FAIL
3. **Keep `FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE`** for FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT (just `RUN`)
4. **Update `FUNCS_DOUBLE`** - split into the two new lists
5. **Update `foreach` calls** - apply the right macro to each list
6. **Update `REF_ELFS`/`OPT_ELFS`** and `test-all` to cover both lists
7. **Update `size` target** to include `S_ASM_OBJS`
8. **Update `clean`** to include `S_ASM_OBJS`

### Also consider
- `s_asinh.s`, `s_atan2.s`, `s_cbrt.s` exist but aren't in `FUNCS_DOUBLE` yet - could add them with test files if those test files get created

  MSG_CC =

file.content:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources — single-precision
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Assembly sources — double-precision
S_ASM_SRCS = s_sin.s s_cos.s s_tan.s s_atan.s s_asin.s s_acos.s s_fabs.s \
             s_ceil.s s_floor.s s_exp.s s_sinh.s s_cosh.s s_tanh.s \
             s_log.s s_log10.s s_sqrt.s s_fmod.s s_pow.s s_frexp.s \
             s_ldexp.s s_modf.s s_fpclassify.s s_expm1.s s_log1p.s \
             s_rint.s s_round.s s_trunc.s s_fmax.s s_fmin.s s_ilogb.s \
             s_nextafter.s s_remquo.s
S_ASM_OBJS = $(S_ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf

# Double-precision functions with s_*.s assembly (opt vs ref comparison)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh \
                   tanh log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
                   expm1 log1p rint round trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter remquo

# Double-precision functions without assembly (run only)
FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT = acosh atanh erf erfc lgamma \
                     j0 j1 jn y0 y1 yn hypot remainder lrint lround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(Q)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(Q)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions with assembly: opt vs ref comparison
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o s_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions without assembly: run only
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(Q)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@printf "  %-14s RUN\n" $(1)
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf) \
           $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size: $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(S_ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

content.text:
Looks correct. Summary of changes:

- Added `S_ASM_SRCS`/`S_ASM_OBJS` for the 32 double-precision assembly files
- Split `FUNCS_DOUBLE` into `FUNCS_DOUBLE_OPT` (32 functions with `s_*.s`) and `FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT` (15 functions without)
- Added `FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE_OPT` macro - same opt/ref diff logic as single-precision, but links `s_$(1).o` directly (no suffix stripping needed)
- `FUNCS_DOUBLE_NOOPT` keeps `RUN` behavior
- `OPT_ELFS`, `size`, and `clean` all updated to cover double-precision asm objects

`make test-cos` will now show `PASS` or `FAIL` instead of `RUN`.
